title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market insights for April 15, 2026 (based on April 14th close for US markets)

items.headline: Wall Street Surges on US-Iran Peace Talk Hopes, S&P 500 Nears All-Time High
items.summary: Optimism surrounding potential renewed US-Iran peace talks fueled a significant rally across Wall Street on Tuesday, April 14, driving major indices sharply higher. The S&P 500 closed within 1% of its record high, while the Nasdaq extended its winning streak to ten consecutive sessions. This positive sentiment was bolstered by easing oil prices and favorable inflation data.

items.headline: Bloom Energy
items.summary: Surged following news of an expanded partnership with Oracle.

items.headline: Oracle
items.summary: Gained on a bullish broader market sentiment due to Iran talks optimism and its expanded partnership with Bloom Energy.

items.headline: Amazon
items.summary: Among the mega-cap tech names driving market gains.

items.headline: Nvidia
items.summary: Continued its powerful run as AI-linked equities extended gains.

items.headline: American Airlines
items.summary: Spiked on reports of a potential merger proposal with United Airlines.

items.headline: Akamai Technologies
items.summary: One of the S&P 500's top losers.

items.headline: WTI Crude Oil
items.summary: Oil prices collapsed due to optimism over renewed US-Iran peace talks.

items.headline: Tech and Financials Lead the Rally, Energy Drags
items.summary: Tech stocks led the market surge, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index gaining 2% and software climbing 1.6%. Financials also joined the broad-based rally. Conversely, the energy sector was the sole decliner, falling 2.2% as crude oil prices plummeted on de-escalation hopes in the Middle East. Industrials and Healthcare are also seen as favorable sectors.

items.headline: Cooling Inflation and Stable Expectations
items.summary: The US Producer Price Index (PPI) for March rose less than expected, with core PPI increasing by only 0.1% monthly, easing concerns about inflation and reducing pressure on the Federal Reserve for further rate hikes. Long-term consumer inflation expectations also remained stable, according to the NY Fed.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports for April 15, 2026
items.summary: ASML Holding NV ($ASML) reported strong Q1 2026 results, beating analyst expectations with a net income of $3.23 billion and revenue of $10.26 billion. BlackRock, Citigroup, and Johnson & Johnson also reported earnings that were well-received by the market. Investors are awaiting results from Bank of America ($BAC), Morgan Stanley ($MS), and PNC Financial Services Group ($PNC) today. Aduro Clean Technologies ($ADUR) and Greystone Logistics ($GLGI) also released their latest earnings.

items.headline: Geopolitical Optimism and Economic Outlooks
items.summary: Hopes for renewed US-Iran peace talks significantly boosted market sentiment and led to a sharp decline in oil prices. While the IMF cut its global growth outlook for 2026 to 3.1% due to the Middle East conflict and warned of potential recession if the conflict escalates, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent maintains a strong outlook for the US economy, projecting growth over 3-3.5%.
